boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Trigger your workflow to notify a set of users

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer sends a notification through a triggered workflow that delivers a message to specified recipients.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: API call to trigger the workflow returns success and the notification is sent to the intended recipients.
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully trigger a workflow to send a notification to users via the Knock API.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This step marks the first time a developer completes the end-to-end flow of creating a workflow and successfully sending a notification, which is the core functional milestone for Knock.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has a backend web application ready for integration
Developer has basic knowledge of API usage and environment variable management
entry_point.developer_intent: Send a first notification using Knock by integrating with the backend and creating a workflow.

friction_gates.description: User must create and log into a Knock account to access the dashboard.
Developer must obtain and correctly configure secret API keys for authentication.
Workflow must have at least one channel step configured to send notifications.
Trigger API call requires correct recipients and data payload to successfully send notifications.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Knock account and log into the Knock dashboard.
Have a backend web application environment ready to integrate with Knock SDK or API.
Obtain secret API keys from the Knock dashboard to authenticate API calls.
Set API keys as environment variables to securely use them in backend integration.

primary_path.action: Create a Knock account and log into the Knock dashboard.
details: Visit Knock website and sign up for an account.
Access the Knock dashboard after login.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account creation errors or login failure
primary_path.success_signal: Dashboard loads and user is authenticated

primary_path.action: Add the Knock SDK to the backend application or prepare to use the API.
details: Choose appropriate SDK for backend language or plan to use direct API calls.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: SDK installation errors or missing dependencies
primary_path.output: SDK installed or API integration planned
primary_path.success_signal: SDK available in project or API client ready

primary_path.action: Retrieve secret API keys from the Knock dashboard and set them as environment variables.
details: Locate API keys under Platform section in dashboard.
Set secret key as environment variable in backend environment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or incorrect API keys cause authentication failure
primary_path.output: Environment variables configured with API keys
primary_path.success_signal: Backend can authenticate API calls to Knock

primary_path.action: Create a workflow in the Knock dashboard.
details: Add at least one channel step to the workflow.
Configure the message template for the channel step.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Errors in workflow creation or invalid channel configuration
primary_path.output: Workflow with configured channel step created
primary_path.success_signal: Workflow appears in dashboard and is editable

primary_path.action: Commit the workflow to the current environment to activate it.
details: Use the Commit button in the workflow canvas header.
Review changes and add commit message.
primary_path.output: Workflow committed and activated in environment

primary_path.action: Trigger the workflow via the Knock API to send a notification.
details: Use the trigger API call with required data: recipients and data payload.
Use sample payload from workflow trigger step as template.
primary_path.input: API call with recipients and data
primary_path.success_signal: API call returns success and notification is delivered

primary_path.action: Publish workflows to production environment when ready.
details: Ensure workflows are in production environment to send real notifications.
Optionally promote workflows from development to production.
primary_path.success_signal: Notifications sent to real users in production

sections_used: What channels can I send notifications on?
sources.title: Documentation | Knock Docs

evidence_supported: Step-by-step instructions to send first notification
sections_used: Add the Knock SDK to your backend
sources.title: Get started with Knock | Knock Docs

evidence_supported: API key usage and environment variables
sources.title: Knock v0.5.0 — Documentation

evidence_supported: Conceptual understanding of Knock messaging infrastructure
sources.title: What is Knock? | Knock Docs

alternatives_considered.name: Knock Elixir SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: While the Elixir SDK is official and documented, the primary onboarding flow documented centers on the dashboard and API usage, which is language-agnostic and more broadly applicable.

surface.name: Knock Dashboard and API
surface.selection_basis: The official Knock documentation emphasizes the use of the Knock dashboard for workflow creation and the Knock API for triggering notifications as the primary developer interaction surfaces.

uncertainties.impact: Time to first success is useful for benchmarking developer onboarding efficiency but is not explicitly documented.
uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending a notification) as claimed or measured by Knock?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit vendor claim or measurement of time to first success found in the official documentation.
